definition:
εἴσ-οδος, -ου, ὁ 
 (ὁδός) [in LXX chiefly for בּוֹא ;] 
  1. a means of entering, place of entrance: Heb.10:19, 2Pe.1:11 (cf. Westc., He., l.with; MM, Exp., xii; but see infr.). 
   2. a going in, entrance: Act.13:24; with genitive loc., Heb.10:19 (Thayer, see word; but see supr.); before εἰς, 2Pe.1:11 (Mayor, in l.; Thayer; but see supr.); πρός, 2Th.1:9 2:1.†
 (AS)
